questionText: Does your organization manage net zero carbon buildings?
questionKo: 귀사는 manage net zero carbon buildings입니까?
simpleHelp: In line with the TCFD recommendations, regulatory measures such as a transition to low-carbon properties may affect the financial viability of existing properties. Understanding the percentage of net zero carbon buildings provides investors with an indication of the potential impact of regulatory measures on your portfolio/ buildings you deliver.
baseOptions: No, but we plan to in the future
No, and we do not plan to in the future

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Buildings management" in response to 1.15.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Buildings 관리" in response to 1.15.

guidance.text:
General 
Indicate whether your organization has net zero carbon buildings under management. If you respond "Yes", you will be requested to provide details on your net zero carbon buildings in the following question.
If you respond "No", you will be requested to explain your organization's plan with regards to managing net zero carbon buildings in the future, or explain why you do not plan to do so.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Indicate whether 귀사 has net zero carbon buildings under 관리. 만약 you respond "예", you will be requested to 세부 정보를 입력 on your net zero carbon buildings in the following 문항. 만약 you respond "No", you will be requested to explain 귀사의 plan with regards to managing net zero carbon buildings in the future, 또는 그 이유를 설명 you do not plan to do so.

guidance.text:
Net zero carbon building : In line with the World Green Building Council's definition, a building that is highly energy efficient and fully powered from on-site and/or off-site renewable energy sources. More specific definitions have been developed by some local Green Building Councils as well as in some national regulations. If you are operating in countries/areas where the definition of net zero carbon building has been defined in a legal document (e.g. building regulations and energy decrees) or by the local Green Building Council, you should apply that definition when responding to this question. Some of the examples of existing/proposed definitions include:
- 
Japan: Net Zero Energy Building (ZEB) : definition proposed by Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry-building with considerably reduced annual energy consumption by saving as much energy as possible via better heat insulation, solar shading, natural energy and high-efficiency equipment as well as creating energy (e.g., with photovoltaic power generation), while maintaining comfortable environments. The goal is to achieve net zero energy consumption by creating [renewable] energy while achieving at least 50% higher energy saving than prescribed by the Energy Saving Standard.
- 
United Kingdom: The Net Zero Carbon Buildings: A Framework Definition: developed by UK Green Building Council sets out definitions and principles around two approaches to net zero carbon - for construction and operational energy, which are of equal importance. Net zero carbon for both construction and operational energy represents the greatest level of commitment to the framework:
- 
Net zero carbon 
- construction : when the amount of carbon emissions associated with a building's product and construction stages up to practical completion is zero or negative, through the use of offsets or the net export of on-site renewable energy;
- 
Net zero carbon 
- operational energy : when the amount of carbon emissions associated with the building's operational energy on an annual basis is zero or negative. A net zero carbon building is highly energy efficient and powered from on-site and/or off-site renewable energy sources, with any remaining carbon balance offset.
guidance.textKo: Net zero carbon building: In line with the World Green Building Council's definition, a building that is highly energy efficient 및 fully powered from on-site 및/또는 off-site 재생에너지 sources. More specific definitions have been developed by some local Green Building Councils as well as in some national regulations. 만약 you are operating in countries/areas 여기서 the definition of net zero carbon building has been defined in a legal document (e.g. building regulations 및 energy decrees) 또는 by the local Green Building Council, you 해야 합니다 apply that definition 경우 responding to this 문항. Some 의 examples of existing/proposed definitions include: - Japan: Net Zero Energy Building (ZEB): definition proposed by Ministry of Economy, Trade 및 Industry-building with considerably reduced annual 에너지 소비량 by saving as much energy as possible via better heat insulation, solar shading, natural energy 및 high-efficiency equipment as well as creating energy (e.g., with photovoltaic 발전), while maintaining comfortable environments. The goal is to achieve net zero 에너지 소비량 by creating [re신규able] energy while achieving 최소 50% higher energy saving than prescribed by the Energy Saving Standard. - United Kingdom: The Net Zero Carbon Buildings: A Framework Definition: developed by UK Green Building Council sets out definitions 및 principles around two approaches to net zero carbon - for construction 및 operational energy, which are of equal importance. Net zero carbon for both construction 및 operational energy represents the greatest level of commitment to the framework: - Net zero carbon - construction: 경우 the amount of carbon emissions associated with a building's product 및 construction stages up to practical completion is zero 또는 negative, through the use of offsets 또는 the net export of on-site 재생에너지; - Net zero carbon - operational energy: 경우 the amount of carbon emissions associated with the building's operational energy on an annual basis is zero 또는 negative. A net zero carbon building is highly energy efficient 및 powered from on-site 및/또는 off-site 재생에너지 sources, with 하나 이상 remaining carbon balance offset.
